--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-06-26) ---
[2018-06-26 08:01] f300xen (f0x): energy, premium currencies, etc
[2018-06-26 08:01] namington: actually, would some mechanic like "Energy persists between turns" break any Prismata design rules that we have laid out?
[2018-06-26 08:02] namington: theres no real precedent to it
[2018-06-26 08:02] namington: nothing else changes a game mechanic like that
[2018-06-26 08:02] namington: but in theory I dont think it goes against any design rules besides "dont change fundamental game mechanics with your units"
[2018-06-26 08:02] crash_overlord: Why not tho. A unit that makes you float energy can be really interesting to play with
[2018-06-26 08:02] xyotsuba: Isn’t that like, the most important one?
[2018-06-26 08:03] crash_overlord: the main problem of it is that it ends up being either too good or way too niche
[2018-06-26 08:03] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): @Namington that's actually an interesting idea
[2018-06-26 08:03] namington: thats the problem with huge, global effect slike that in general
[2018-06-26 08:03] namington: in some sets its really good
[2018-06-26 08:03] xyotsuba: I guess u can argue that that isn’t a fundamental mechanic?
[2018-06-26 08:03] namington: in others its useless
[2018-06-26 08:03] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): would break SDR if it were two-sided though
[2018-06-26 08:03] namington: I mean take something like
[2018-06-26 08:03] f300xen (f0x): wasn't the gift scrapped for a reason
[2018-06-26 08:03] crash_overlord: how would it break SDR
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: "Your Energy persists between turns. Click and pay :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: :Energy: to gain 3 :Gold: and 3 :Attack: "
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: numbers are fairly arbitrary
[2018-06-26 08:04] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): because having it would benefit your opponent
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: but like smthn like that has the problem of
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: being obviously really good in sets iwth engi gen
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: like hellhound or w/e
[2018-06-26 08:04] f300xen (f0x): that's not what SDR is
[2018-06-26 08:04] namington: and otherwise too stringent on its gimmick
[2018-06-26 08:04] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): and not in a Valkyrie kind of way
[2018-06-26 08:04] crash_overlord: Actually yeah it kinda is fox
[2018-06-26 08:04] crash_overlord: Sometimes you'd rather not have the unit than have it
[2018-06-26 08:05] shadourow (Rando): SDR mean having it is better than not having it, if it help your opponent that can break SDR
[2018-06-26 08:05] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): or a Cluster Bolt kind of way
[2018-06-26 08:05] xyotsuba: Cluster bolt is a spell tho so u can choose not to use it
[2018-06-26 08:06] xyotsuba: If cluster bolt was a 4 attacker that made a gauss charge for opponent every turn it would break sdr
[2018-06-26 08:06] namington: "When bought, construct 6 Galvani Drones with :Time: 3. Your Energy persists between turns." could be another example mechanic that makes use of it
[2018-06-26 08:06] namington: which might be a bit less set-dependent
[2018-06-26 08:07] namington: its still really hard to balance tho so probably not a good idea
[2018-06-26 08:07] shadourow (Rando): Oh ! I like this idea !
[2018-06-26 08:07] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Would make sense for a cheap 1 supply unit
[2018-06-26 08:08] crash_overlord: I'd rather make it smaller so that it enables more opening without going 3rd engy
[2018-06-26 08:08] crash_overlord: That's the main part of floating energy that's interesting imo
[2018-06-26 08:08] shadourow (Rando): That could be really interesting to make energy a valuable non-early game ressource
[2018-06-26 08:09] shadourow (Rando): (I mean, another unit for that, there is already few units which do that)
[2018-06-26 08:13] leary93: On cryo kronus: Yes the idea is that if you dont click it to chill, it won't use chill (compulsive prob needs to be clearer worded). End turn with this unit in play would just put you in breach phase if that's applicable. And since when can't green have shock? That's very green if anything.
[2018-06-26 08:13] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Green is the burst damage color anyway
[2018-06-26 08:14] f300xen (f0x): green has no shock other than nivo
[2018-06-26 08:15] f300xen (f0x): clusterbolt is 0 shock since it costs 4 green for 4 attack
[2018-06-26 08:15] namington: clusterbolt doesnt exist
[2018-06-26 08:15] leary93: Also, why is 1 attack and 1 chill shcok >.<
[2018-06-26 08:16] leary93: why is shock tied to tech?
[2018-06-26 08:17] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): wait green isn't burst damage?
[2018-06-26 08:17] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): then wth is iso kronus doing

[2018-06-26 08:17] leary93: and if that's true, doesnt iso qualify for 2 threat on 1 tech?
[2018-06-26 08:17] leary93: I thought shock is more like: initial high damage, with a dropoff
[2018-06-26 08:18] leary93: like, I'd qualify scorch as shock, valk etc
[2018-06-26 08:18] crash_overlord: shock is clasified as threat/damage being higher than tech cost
[2018-06-26 08:19] f300xen (f0x): but that makes iceblade shock smh
[2018-06-26 08:19] crash_overlord: if you take a quick peek most units have equal damage/threat to tech ratio
[2018-06-26 08:19] leary93: Ye but then iso is shock right? even tho bt2
[2018-06-26 08:19] crash_overlord: There's very few units that have more than the cost
[2018-06-26 08:19] crash_overlord: and a few more that have less
[2018-06-26 08:19] crash_overlord: Not sure, probably yes.
[2018-06-26 08:20] f300xen (f0x): there are actually quite a few that have shock iirc
[2018-06-26 08:20] crash_overlord: I think it's the fewest of the 3 posibilites
[2018-06-26 08:20] f300xen (f0x): yeah definitely the fewest
[2018-06-26 08:20] crash_overlord: oh dam, nerfdeemer :monkaS:
[2018-06-26 08:20] crash_overlord: Hm lemme go count them out now I'm curious
[2018-06-26 08:20] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Burst damage I see as being able to do big damage only some of the time, Wincer is the most burst damage unit, then Tia
[2018-06-26 08:21] f300xen (f0x): synth is the most burst damage unit obviously
[2018-06-26 08:21] crash_overlord: Ok are we counting stuff like antima as attackers
[2018-06-26 08:21] leary93: TIL proto isnt even shock
[2018-06-26 08:21] crash_overlord: Also now that I think about it I'm pretty sure buildtime cancels out shock
[2018-06-26 08:22] crash_overlord: shock meaning you can buy more attack than you have tech this turn. So having 1 tech and creating 2+ damage for the next turn is seen as shock
[2018-06-26 08:22] crash_overlord: I'm unsure if the same can be said for stuff like iso
[2018-06-26 08:22] crash_overlord: because it's not for specifically next turn
[2018-06-26 08:22] leary93: Is bloodrager and arka shock?
[2018-06-26 08:22] crash_overlord: but dunno, not sure about the exact origin of the shock term in prismata tbh
[2018-06-26 08:22] leary93: is chill considered into shock?
[2018-06-26 08:23] leary93: it seems supervague and I dont understand how Cryo kronus isn't totally green
[2018-06-26 08:23] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): tatsu is not shock no matter what you say
[2018-06-26 08:24] f300xen (f0x): iceblade, vai, tatsu, shiver yeti, arka, rager, fire spinner, alloy swirler, ampo, nivo, redeemer, odin, gmech, drake, tia is a full list i think
[2018-06-26 08:24] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): and neither is vai (which is not @Rorvul)
[2018-06-26 08:29] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): also Arka is the exact opposite of shock
[2018-06-26 08:29] leary93: But attack isn't tech, so it's shock 🤔
[2018-06-26 08:29] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): Arka is the furthest thing from shock
[2018-06-26 08:29] valgrim_the_demon (Rorvul): if Arka were shock, so would Blastforge
[2018-06-26 08:29] f300xen (f0x): have you heard of "end-user discretion"
[2018-06-26 08:30] mrguy888: origin of shock is masn
[2018-06-26 08:31] mrguy888: For practical purposes rager and arka are not shock
[2018-06-26 08:31] crash_overlord: Amporilla, Antima*, arka, bloodrager, drake, gmech, iceblade, lancetooth*, redeemer, scorchila*,  tatsu, tia, vai, zemora*, iso*, wincer*, odin for shock (* marks buildtime > 1). That's 11 buildtime 1 shock.

Exact: Splitter, Gauss, Rhino, Tarsier, apollo (3 tech, 3 damage), arms race, bloodphage, borehole, dmech, vore, flanimus, hanibull, grimbotch, immolite, kinetic, militia, mahar, lucina, nitrocybe, pixie, gauss charge, omega, perf, plasmafier, protoplasm, shredder, smorcus, valkyrion, xaetron, tantalum. That's 30 not counting apollo I think.

Anti-shock or more tech than threat: Xeno, Sentry, Thunderhead, Sentinel, Shadowfang,  manticore, hellhound, Symbiote, Feral warden, Cynestra, Centurion, Chieftain, Cauterizer, Asteri Cannon. That's 15

[2018-06-26 08:31] namington: steelsplitter off steelforge is shock
[2018-06-26 08:32] crash_overlord: I listed them both cause I wasn't sure

[2018-06-28 01:53] kaelii: As it is, a Cryopot train costs 3 red to setup and then continues to provide :Attack:, :Chill: 2 and 2 soak each turn for 4 gold.
[2018-06-28 01:55] kaelii: Which, isn't spectacular damage/threat but is fine soak. Its possible it might need a tiny buff to make it slightly more appealing soak.

[2018-06-28 01:57] kaelii: If you just want to use cryopots for triple red units (fang mostly?) you get two turns of triple red off of one turn of 0 red.
